terms.definition: A paradigm shift where workflows are built around the capabilities of autonomous AI agents rather than human-centric legacy systems. These agents dynamically learn and optimize end-to-end sequences, interacting with disparate data sets and other agents to execute entire business functions without manual intervention.
terms.analogy: In a kitchen, traditional automation is a dishwasher with three buttons; agentic redesign is a robotic head chef that monitors inventory, decides the nightly specials based on market prices, and orchestrates the sous-chefs to deliver the meal.
terms.business_value: Shifts the bottom line from incremental efficiency to exponential scalability. By removing 'fragmented legacy' bottlenecks, CEOs can transition their workforce from task execution to high-level outcome management.

terms.definition: A formal verification algorithm used to mathematically prove that a system will never enter an 'unsafe' state during operation. It iteratively analyzes state transitions to ensure that safety properties are maintained, even in complex hardware or software environments where manual testing is impossible.
terms.analogy: It is like a car's stability control system that doesn't just react to a skid, but uses a mathematical model to ensure that no combination of steering and braking inputs could ever physically allow the car to flip over.
terms.business_value: Critical for de-risking high-stakes deployments in aerospace, automotive, or fintech. AI-enhanced IC3 (like IC3-Evolve) dramatically reduces the time and cost of safety certification, accelerating the time-to-market for mission-critical hardware.

terms.definition: An evaluation framework that moves beyond aggregate 'accuracy percentages' to analyze AI performance on a granular, individual-item basis. This exposes 'systemic validity failures' where a model might have a high average score but fails consistently on specific types of high-stakes logic or edge cases.
terms.analogy: Imagine a car safety rating that doesn't just give a '5-star' average, but details exactly how the car performs at every specific speed, angle, and weather condition, ensuring there isn't a hidden flaw that only appears during a left turn in the rain.
terms.business_value: Protects the brand from 'catastrophic outliers.' For an executive, this provides the granular evidence required to deploy GenAI in regulated environments, ensuring the system is reliable for every customer interaction, not just 'most' of them.
